title: Fix gender theme persistence/color binding and update Home hero image + remove overlay

requirements.summary: Ensure Baby Girl/Baby Boy selection updates Tailwind-driven site colors across routes and persists after refresh/navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking “Baby Girl” updates the overall site color palette immediately (e.g., background, primary/secondary/accent-based UI elements).
Clicking “Baby Boy” updates the overall site color palette immediately.
The selected theme persists on page refresh and when navigating between routes.
The theme implementation remains compatible with Tailwind’s `hsl(var(--...))` color bindings (i.e., CSS variable values are in a format Tailwind can resolve correctly).

file_operations.description: Update theme CSS variables (neutral/dark/girl/boy) to use `hsl(var(--...))`-compatible values (space-separated HSL triplets / optional alpha) and ensure all core tokens (background/foreground/card/popover/primary/secondary/accent/muted/border/input/ring/destructive/etc.) are set for each gender theme so colors apply consistently across pages.

Harden theme application so the `data-gender` attribute is applied reliably on initial load and on theme changes (and continues persisting to localStorage) to prevent theme reset/flash during refresh or navigation.

Adjust the Baby Girl/Baby Boy selector styling to rely on theme tokens (Tailwind classes using the CSS-variable-driven palette) rather than hard-coded pink/blue so buttons and surrounding UI reflect the selected gender theme consistently. Do not modify any files under `frontend/src/components/ui`.

requirements.summary: Replace the Home page hero illustration with the uploaded `sioj.png` static asset and apply a lightweight animation.
acceptanceCriteria: `sioj.png` is added as a static frontend asset (served from `frontend/public/...`) and is used as the Home page hero image in place of the current hero illustration.
The hero image has a visible, kid-friendly, lightweight animation (e.g., existing `animate-float` or `animate-bounce-gentle`) that does not harm readability/performance.
The image renders once (no duplicate rendering of the same hero image).

file_operations.description: Add the uploaded `sioj.png` to the static public assets so it can be referenced by the Home page hero image.

Replace the current hero illustration source with `/assets/sioj.png` and apply an existing lightweight animation utility class (e.g., `animate-float` or `animate-bounce-gentle`) while ensuring the hero image is rendered only once.

requirements.summary: Remove the unintended overlay/duplicate image layered above the Home hero image.
acceptanceCriteria: On the affected page(s), there is no longer any unintended/extra image positioned on top of the main hero image.
The intended main image remains visible and properly aligned after removal.
No layout overlap or z-index stacking causes a second image to cover the main image.

file_operations.description: Remove or refactor the extra/overlay image element(s) in the hero area that layer above the main hero illustration (e.g., decorative/mascot overlay) and verify positioning/z-index so only the intended hero image remains visible and aligned.
